TextENGNew:
It was one afternoon after school, barely past the start of July,
on a day with all the ferocity of midsummer heat.
"...Mii! Everyone, lend me your ears for a moment."
"What's on your mind, Rika-chan?"
"Mii! I want to go to the pool with everyone."
"Huh... the pool?"
It was right in the middle of cleaning up after club activities that Rika-chan suddenly brought it up.
It seemed sudden at first, but come to think of it, Rika-chan had been mentioning it here and there for a while now.
July had only just begun.
Summer break was still a little ways off, but whether it was abnormal weather or not, the days had been running at full midsummer intensity since the very start of July.
So it was no mystery that Rika-chan would start yearning for the pool.

"Now that you mention it, Rika-chan has been saying she wanted to go to the pool for a while.
Hmm, the pool, huh... I have to admit, in this heat it does sound awfully tempting~."

"Hau! That sounds wonderful, absolutely wonderful!
Rena has absolutely no objections whatsoever♪"

"Oh, absolutely.
Not that the stream isn't fun, but sometimes one wishes to swim with abandon in a proper pool."

"Come to think of it, this school doesn't have a pool.
How do you handle swimming class?"
changeReason: Old asks 'How did you all learn how to swim?' while new asks 'How do you handle swimming class?'; the Japanese プール授業とかはどうしてるんだ specifically asks about swimming class logistics, not about learning to swim.

TextENGNew:
"We play in the stream, and go to the community pool in Okinomiya together.
They finished renovating it last year.
Apparently it's become quite a nice place to splash around now."
"...Mii! The lazy river and water slides sound wonderfully exciting."

"I'm so excited!
Hey, Mii-chan — what would everyone say to going to the pool this Sunday, this Sunday?"

"This guy doesn't have any part-time shifts lined up.
Anyone who can't make it this Sunday?"
"Rena is free."
"I'm available as well."
"Of course, nipah~☆"
Nobody had any conflicts.

"Oh, oh! So what's this — we're going to the pool this Sunday?!
Hehe, now that's something to look forward to!"

"Oh ho ho ho! How delightful!
I've been positively aching to see this newly renovated pool — oh, it's going to be marvelous!"
"...Splashing about in the water with everyone. I can hardly wait."

"Heh, I'd be thrilled if it were just innocent splashing around.
But knowing you all, there's bound to be some outrageous club activity waiting for us at the pool, isn't there?"

"Ha ha ha, who's to say?
In this heat the pool will be packed with ordinary folk.
If it's that crowded we might have to hold ourselves back.
We can figure it out when we get there."

"That's true.
Rena thinks just taking it easy at the pool without any club games would be perfectly lovely, wouldn't it?"
"...Mii. That would be quite enough fun for me as well."

"Ha ha, once in a while something like that does sound nice.
Forget the daily bloodshed and just let ourselves unwind a bit — there's nothing wrong with that."

"Huh, that's unlike you.
I was dead certain you already had some horrific punishment game all prepared and ready to go."
...For once, it seemed like we might actually get to spend a laid-back day together.
...Still, I couldn't afford to let my guard down.
Knowing this group, the day would surely descend into chaos one way or another.

"Oh ho ho ho!
With so many strangers about, conducting oneself with propriety is nothing more than common sense~!"

"The day has come when I hear Satoko utter the phrase 'common sense.'
Is a storm on the way?"
"Wh-what was that?!"

"All right, all right.
And so, as club president, I — Mion Sonozaki — hereby accept Rika-chan's proposal!
This Sunday, everyone assembles at the community pool!
Don't forget your swimsuit, towel, and wallet~!"
"Yeaaaaah!!"
The pool!
The last time I'd actually been in the water was a swimming lesson at my old school last summer, I think.
School swimming classes were stiff and stuffy affairs, but going with the club? Whether or not it stayed relaxed, it was bound to be a blast!
I counted the days to Sunday on my fingers, buzzing with anticipation.
"Au, au, I'm so looking forward to it~♪"
"...You're coming too, Hanyuu?"
"Au~, please don't leave me out~."

"It's not like I'm excluding you...
But even if you come, nobody can see you. Won't that be dreadfully dull?"

"Not at all!
Watching Keiichi and everyone playing happily, and then watching you all writhing in the aftermath of some unforeseen catastrophe — that's plenty entertaining in its own right~ au♪"

"...Part of that is a little concerning, but fine.
Just don't go clomping around making footstep noises everywhere. Behave yourself."
"Yes, of course. Au☆"
